text: In the kingdom of Wyverngarde,[p/] there was a gulf between what was lawful and what was just.
translation: 在维文加德王国，[p/]法律的规定与正义的真谛之间，存在着一条鸿沟。

text: Artificery ran in the houses of the aristocrats.[p/] Disease and vermin ran through the homes of the commonfolk.
translation: 奥术能源充盈着贵族府邸。[p/]疾病与害虫在百姓家中肆虐。

text: People suffered,[p/] people died,[p/] all so the mages of the nobility could keep their plates full and their lives comfortable.
translation: 平民们受苦，[p/]平民们死去，[p/]这都仅仅为了让那些贵族法师们吃饱穿暖，生活无忧。

text: Nevertheless,[p/] they still wanted us to have the illusion of hope.
translation: 尽管如此，[p/]他们仍然想让我们保有一丝希望的假象。

text: They challenged us to win,[p/] leaving out the fact that they were the ones setting the rules.
translation: 他们鼓动我们去争取胜利，[p/]却对规则本身就是他们一手制定的事实避而不谈。

text: Many just threw up their hands,[p/] and no one could blame them.
translation: 许多人干脆举手投降了，[p/]这也无从指摘。

text: You could never lose if you never played.
translation: 毕竟你只要不搅和进去，才永远不会输掉。

text: Rhys: I was transporting a client in my carriage that evening.
translation: Rhys: 那晚，我正载着一位客人。

text: Rhys: We were driving by the McCoy Tavern when we heard a disturbance.
translation: Rhys: 我们路过麦考伊的酒馆时，[p/]听到了一些吵闹声。

text: Rhys: Worried that something was amiss,[p/] I walked into the establishment to see what the commotion was about.
translation: Rhys: 由于担心会出什么事，[p/]我走进了酒馆一探究竟。

text: Rhys: That was when I saw the defendant,[p/] lit by the blue glow of the dining room lights![p/] She had stabbed Flinhart,[p/][s/] and her sword was shining with magical energy!
translation: Rhys: 就在那时我看见了被告。[p/]餐厅里蔚蓝的微光照亮了她的身影。她当时正刺向弗林哈特，[p/][s/]而且她的剑上闪耀着魔法的光芒！

text: Tyrion: Mr. Rudolf,[p/] you testified that you saw my client murdering the victim,[p/] and you supposedly saw her casting magic while doing so.
translation: Tyrion: 鲁道夫先生，[p/]你作证看到我的委托人在杀害被害者。[p/]而且据你所说还看到她同时在施放法术。

text: Rhys: Yes...[p/] I could never forget it.[p/] It was burned into my memory.
translation: Rhys: 是的……[p/]我不可能记错。[p/]那深深地烙印在我的记忆中。

text: Tyrion: I see...[p/] It’s funny that you say that.
Rhys: ...What?
translation: Rhys:……什么意思？

text: Tyrion: Because your entire testimony was a complete lie!
translation: Tyrion: 因为你所有的证言都是彻头彻尾的谎话！

text: Rhys: W-[p/][s/]What?!
translation: Rhys: 什、[p/][s/]什么？！

text: AriaC: What are you trying to imply, Mr. Cuthbert?
translation: AriaC: 你想暗示什么呢，卡斯伯特先生？

text: Tyrion: Steelwind,[p/] think about the state of the crime scene!
translation: Tyrion: 斯蒂尔文德，[p/]仔细想想犯罪现场的情况！

text: Tyrion: The witness couldn’t have seen [i]anything[/i],[p/] much less a homicide!
Maybe their rules and institutions [i]were[/i] impossible.
Maybe I was just a grain of sand trying to stand against an ocean.
translation: 也许我只是一粒沙子，却妄图与整片海洋抗衡。

text: But I would never stop rising—[p/]no matter how many times they tried to drown me.
translation: 但不论他们多少次想要将我淹没[p/]，我永远不会放弃冲出水面。

text: I would do it again and again if it I meant that I could give hope to even one person.
translation: 哪怕仅仅能为一人带来希望，我都会一次又一次地尝试。

text: [i]That[/i] was why I fought.
Sweat was soaking through my suit.[p/] The air of the countryside was choking me.
translation: 汗水已经浸透了我的衬衫。[p/]乡村的气息让我窒息。

text: I fumbled with the leather reins,[p/] tugging at them tightly.
translation: 我笨拙地摆弄着皮缰绳，[p/]把它拉拽得绷紧了。

text: Horse: Where'd this idiot learn how to drive!?
translation: Horse: 这傻子到底在哪学的驾车？

text: Animals were funny like that.[p/] You'd never know it,[p/] but they were constantly making their objections known.
translation: 动物们总是这么有趣。[p/]你只是从来没法知晓，[p/]但它们其实一直在表达反对意见。

text: I'd caught many complaints like that through my Eye of Horus.
translation: 我通过我的荷鲁斯之眼收到了很多类似的抱怨。

text: Ruby: Cuthbert, keep the carriage steady.
translation: Ruby: 卡斯伯特，把车开稳一点。

text: Tyrion: Y-[p/][s/]Yes, Ms. Tymora.
translation: Tyrion: 好、[p/][s/]好的，泰摩拉女士。

text: My mentor barely looked up from her Runestone.[p/] She was staring at some magical projections—[p/]probably some court files from a case she was working on.
translation: 我的导师几乎就没从她的符石上抬起过视线。[p/]她一直盯着某些法术投影——[p/]大概是她正经手案件的一些法庭记录。

text: I stared longer than I should have.[p/] I'd just passed the legal certification exam,[p/] which only meant that a Runestone of my own was coming my way.
translation: 我盯着看了太久。[p/]我前不久通过了司法资格考试，[p/]这意味着属于我的符石也快到手了。

text: I just had to prove myself,[p/] and this trip was the perfect opportunity.
translation: 我需要证明自己，[p/] 而这次出差正是完美的机会。

text: Dissidence was brewing in the countryside,[p/] and we were on our way to meet with one of the voices of the revolution.
translation: 不满的情绪在乡村不停地酝酿，[p/]而我们正要去会见其中一位革命领袖。

text: The village we were driving to was nearly at the edge of the eastern border,[p/] far from the capital city in the kingdom's center.
translation: 我们驶向的村庄已经接近东部边境，[p/]与王国中心的首都距离遥远。

text: Here,[p/] we ran the risk of running into the military forces deployed to silence voices like his.
translation: 在这里，[p/]我们得冒着与那些想让他闭嘴的军队撞个正着的风险。

text: Whoever this "Flinhart McCoy" was,[p/] Ms. Tymora’s business with him must've been worth all this trouble.
translation: 不管这个“弗林哈特·麦考伊”是什么人，[p/]泰摩拉女士与他的生意一定值得这些付出。

text: Tyrion: M-[p/][s/]Ms. Tymora,[p/] maybe you should switch with me.
translation: Tyrion: 泰、[p/][s/]泰摩拉女士，[p/]可能您得跟我换下手。

text: Ruby: And what would you learn if I did that?
translation: Ruby: 这样的话你又学得到什么呢？

text: ...Maybe I [i]was[/i] worried about nothing.[p/] Practical experience was always a key part of learning whenever she was involved.
Why else would she quietly sit there,[p/] immersed in her court files?
translation: 要不然她为什么只是静静地坐在一边，[p/]埋头研究她的庭审记录呢？

text: Child: Agh!
translation: Child: 啊！

text: ...Did that rock just scream out in pain?
translation: ……是那块石头刚刚发出了痛苦的叫声吗？

text: I grabbed the reins and pulled them with everything I had.[p/] The horses slowed to a stop.
translation: 我抓紧缰绳，用尽全身力气拉拽，[p/]马匹慢慢停了下来。

text: The child was lying face down in the dirt.[p/] A puddle of red was pooling under her.
translation: 一个小孩面朝下地倒在泥土里。[p/]她下方积着一摊红色的东西。

text: Tyrion: Scaled Lord...
translation: Tyrion: 鳞主在上……

text: I wasn't sure what to do.[p/] I wished I'd never procrastinated reading those first-aid books.
translation: 我不知道该怎么办。[p/]真后悔当初一直拖着，没去学习那些急救的书籍。

text: I looked to my mentor.[p/] She'd approached the scene quietly and slowly.
translation: 我看向我的导师，[p/]她沉默而缓慢地走近事发现场。

text: Her eyes went to the child,[p/] then to the surrounding forest.[p/] She seemed to be searching for something.
translation: 她看向那个小孩，[p/]然后扫视四周的丛林。[p/]她看起来在找什么东西。

text: She was wearing the same expression she used when inspecting a crime scene.
translation: 她脸上摆出一副调查犯罪现场时同样的表情。

text: Ruby: ...Why would a kid be out in the middle of nowhere?
translation: Ruby: ……一个小孩子怎么出现在这荒郊野外的？

text: Tyrion: ...Ms. Tymora?
translation: Tyrion: ……泰摩拉女士？

text: Ruby: I say we keep driving and never speak of this again.
translation: Ruby: 要我说，我们就继续开车，这件事不用再提了。

text: Tyrion: Ms. Tymora![p/][s/] She’s just a child!
translation: Tyrion: 泰摩拉女士！[p/][s/]她还只是个小孩子啊！

text: Child: Oh...[p/] The pain... [p/]I can see the light... [h="Where the hell is he?|That was his cue." e="h"/]
translation: Child: 呃……[p/]好疼……[p/]我好像看到光了……[h="那家伙死哪儿去了？|这可是他的活儿" e="h"/]

text: ...I caught a puzzling thought through my Eye of Horus.
translation: ……我的荷鲁斯之眼捕捉到令人费解的想法。

text: Late to his cue,[p/] a man clad in plate armor leaped out of some nearby bushes and stumbled towards us.
translation: 迟疑之间，[p/]一个身着板甲的男子从附近的树丛中跃起，晃晃荡荡地向我们走来。

text: He planted his feet firmly on the ground in front of us,[p/] puffing out his chest.
translation: 他随后在我们面前站定，[p/]高高挺起了胸膛。

text: Knight: By the Scaled Lord![p/][s/] Look what you’ve done!
translation: Knight: 以鳞甲领主之名！[p/][s/]看看你们干的好事！

text: Child: T-[s/][p/]The Scaled Lord...?[p/] I think I can see him.
translation: Child: 鳞、[s/][p/]鳞甲领主……？[p/]我好像都看到祂了……

text: Child: My family—[p/]who all died of the plague in 589—[p/]they're also there.
translation: Child: 我的家人——[p/]他们在589年的瘟疫中死去——[p/]他们也在那里啊。

text: ...Her lines were historically accurate.
translation: ……她的台词倒是对得上史实。

text: Knight: ... [h="Damn it, Alma.|Mentioning that plague is overkill..." e="f"/]
translation: Knight: [h="该死的，阿尔玛。|提到那场瘟疫可做过头了……" e="f"/]

text: Knight: ...This poor child![p/][s/] She’s [i]dead[/i] because of you!
Knight: This is a serious crime![p/][s/] The fine is 10[p/]—no,[p/][s/] [i]20[/i] gold pieces! [h="Look at that carriage.|They’re probably aristocrats visiting House Pierce." e="h"/]
20 gold pieces?[p/] The average sentence for vehicular manslaughter was two years of imprisonment.
translation: 20枚金币？[p/]交通事故过失杀人一般是处两年有期徒刑。

text: Knight: I’ll have no choice but to arrest you unless you can pay this fine!
translation: Knight: 我别无选择，直到你们付清罚金前，必须要拘捕你们！

text: Ruby: Oh,[p/] [i]that's[/i] what this is...
I side-eyed the child—[p/]apparently named Alma.[p/] I spotted tiny tomato seeds in her pool of blood.
translation: 我向那个小孩子瞟了一眼——[p/]显然她叫作阿尔玛，[p/]我发现她身下的“血泊”中有几粒番茄种子。

text: Tyrion: ...You might want to call someone to retrieve Alma’s body then.
translation: Tyrion: ……那你可能还想喊人把阿尔玛的尸体运走呢。

text: Her eyes opened,[p/] and her head whipped up to look at me.
translation: 她睁开眼睛，[p/]抬头看向我。

text: We locked eyes for a second before her head fell back to the ground.
translation: 我们视线相对一刻，她赶紧又将头垂倒在地。

text: I looked to my mentor again,[p/] but she just leaned her head and gestured towards the two con artists.
translation: 我又看向我的导师，[p/]她只是歪着头，朝那两个骗子做了个手势。

text: It appeared that I was being given another learning opportunity.
translation: 看起来我又得到一次学习的机会。

text: I couldn't complain, though.[p/] I didn’t appreciate being treated like an easy mark.
translation: 我不打算抱怨。[p/]因为我也不想被人觉得容易上当受骗。

text: Knight: A-[p/][s/]Anyways, what will it be?!
translation: Knight: 总、[p/][s/]总之。你到底要怎么办？！

text: Knight: Will you pay your fine,[p/] or do we need to sort this out at the barracks?
translation: Knight: 你是乖乖交出罚金，[p/]还是跟我到营房里把事情搞清楚？

text: Negotiate [c="This was obviously just an accident. Can’t you overlook this?"/]
translation: Negotiate [c="这显然只是个意外。能否就此放过？"/]

text: Tyrion: This was obviously just an accident.[p/] Can’t you look the other way?
translation: Tyrion: 这显然只是个意外。[p/]能不能当做没有发生过呢？

text: Knight: Are you insane?![p/][s/] You want me to spit on my [i]honor[/i] as a knight?!
Ruby: ...What are you doing, Cuthbert?
translation: Ruby: 卡斯伯特，你到底在干嘛？

text: Tyrion: They clearly worked very hard on this.[p/] I thought it would be rude not to play along.
translation: Tyrion: 他们干得这么辛苦。[p/]我觉得不配合一下就太煞风景了。

text: Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Tyrion: ...Sorry.
translation: Tyrion: ……对不起。

text: Comply [c="Okay, I guess we can spare that much gold."/]
translation: Comply [c="好吧，我想我们付得起这笔钱。"/]

text: Tyrion: Okay,[p/] I guess we can spare that much gold.
translation: Tyrion: 好吧，[p/]我想我们付得起这笔钱。

text: Ruby: ...Cuthbert,[p/] what are you doing?
translation: Ruby: ……卡斯伯特，你干什么呢？

text: Tyrion: I just thought arguing with them would be more trouble than it’s worth.
translation: Tyrion: 我就是觉得与他们争吵可能导致比这笔钱更大的损失。

text: Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Tyrion: ...Sorry.
translation: Tyrion: ……对不起。

text: Call [c="Certainly, why don’t you bring us back to your \"barracks\"?"/]
translation: Call [c="好啊，那你干嘛不把我们带回你的“营房”?"/]

text: Tyrion: Certainly,[p/] why don’t you bring us back to your "barracks"?
translation: Tyrion: 好啊，[p/]那你干嘛不把我们带回你的“营房”?

text: Knight: What—?
translation: Knight: 你——？

text: Tyrion: But it [i]is[/i] strange that you’d bring us to your barracks instead of the dungeons.
Knight: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/][s/] Uh—
translation: Knight: 噢、[p/][s/]哦啊……[p/][s/]呃——

text: Tyrion: You’ll also probably want to call your unit to escort us.
translation: Tyrion: 你大概还得召唤你的部队押送我们。

text: Tyrion: After all,[p/] it'd be dangerous for one knight to transport two "criminals" by himself.
translation: Tyrion: 毕竟呢，[p/]仅仅一名骑士独自押送两名“罪犯”还是有些危险。

text: Knight: S-[p/][s/]Shut up![p/][s/] Don't think you can talk your way out of this!
translation: Knight: 闭、[p/][s/]闭嘴！[p/][s/]别以为你三言两语就想脱身！

text: Knight: Are you seriously going to make me take you in?![p/][s/] Just pay the damned fine!
translation: Knight: 你还真想让我把你抓进去？！[p/][s/]快把你的钱给我交了！

text: Vehicular Manslaughter
translation: 交通肇事过失致死

text: Tyrion
translation: 提利昂

text: Knight: ...Who is this?[p/] This is you, isn’t it?
translation: Knight: ……这是谁？[p/]这不就是你，对吧？

text: Tyrion: It sure is.
translation: Tyrion: 确实就是。

text: Knight: ...
translation: Knight: ……

text: Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: Ruby
translation: 鲁比

text: Knight: ...Who is this?[p/] Isn’t this the lady standing beside you?
Tyrion: It sure is.
translation: Tyrion: 确实就是。

text: Knight: ...
translation: Knight: ……

text: Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: Tyrion: Except a "crime" like this wouldn’t involve a fine at all.
Knight: ...What?
Tyrion: Vehicular manslaughter has an average sentence of two years of imprisonment.
Tyrion: With the death of a child, though,[p/] I could see the Crown pushing for more than that.
Knight: ...Who the hell are you?!
Tyrion: I’m a defense attorney.[p/] I could recite every one of the kingdom's laws back to you.
Knight: ...
translation: Knight: ……

text: The "knight" looked nervously between Ms. Tymora and me.
Knight: ...That’s it!
His hand shot to the rusty sword hanging from his side.
He fumbled with it for a bit before pulling it out and waving it at us.
I took a step back.[p/] My mentor put herself in front of me.
Knight: Enough talk![p/][s/] Give us the gold,[p/] and I won't have to hurt either of you! [h="Come on! Give up!|I can’t move that well in this fake armor..." e="f"/]
Logic
Intuition
Empathy
Tyrion: What you’re saying doesn’t match what you’re thinking.
Knight: What am I "thinking"?[p/] What are you talking about?
Ruby: Cuthbert,[p/] now is not the time to bluff!
Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh, [p/][s/]uh—
[i]He[/i] was the one that was bluffing.[p/] I just needed to call him out on it.
I stared at his plate armor.[p/] On a closer look,[p/] it seemed to be varnished wood,[p/] painted to give off a metallic sheen.
I reached past his sword and knocked on his breastplate.[p/] It let out a hollow thunk instead of a resonant clang.
Knight: Uh...
Tyrion: I didn’t know the kingdom was cutting back.[p/] Would this even protect you in a fight?
Knight: A-[p/][s/]Are you threatening me?
Tyrion: With litigation, perhaps.
Knight: I-[p/][s/]I have a sword!
Tyrion: You won't use it.
Knight: I—
Tyrion: Look...[p/] Let's just cut the charade.[p/] It's not happening.
Knight: ...
translation: Knight: ……

text: Alma rose from the dead and began wiping the rotten, crushed tomatoes off her shirt.
Child: Sorry,[p/] I thought that stuff about the plague would help... [h="I'm tired of eating porridge..." e="sf"/]
Knight: Don’t worry about it...[p/] It’s still morning.[p/] Someone else will come by.
Child: The road’s been empty for days.[p/] What if they’re the only ones?
Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: :The Kingdom of Wyverngarde:[p/] established 613 years ago by the seven high archmages—[p/]ruled by King Olivier and the mages of his nobility.
They'll often preach that our large kingdom was a land of prosperity and happiness—[p/]a nation where its people shared the wealth and succor created by its magic.
Tyrion: ...Here.
I took out a neatly wrapped sandwich from my satchel and held it out to Alma.
Child: W-[p/]What...?
Tyrion: You’ll probably find this more filling than porridge.
Knight: ...
translation: Knight: ……

text: The man narrowed his eyes,[p/] appraising me like the knight he'd been impersonating.
He snatched the wrapped sandwich from me,[p/] peeling back the bread to inspect its contents.
His mouth salivated as he caught a whiff of the sliced deli meat.
No poison to be found, apparently.[p/] He handed it to Alma,[p/] who eagerly began munching down on it.
I was probably the first person to share [i]any[/i] "wealth or succor" with them in a long time.
Knight: ...Just tell me one thing.
Knight: What gave it away?
Tyrion: ...What do you mean?
Knight: I spent a while sanding this armor down.[p/] I had to go through three attempts just to get it perfectly smooth.
Knight: I even mixed the paint myself.[p/] It's got the sheen and everything.
Knight: How'd you know it was fake?
I thought to myself for a while.
Tyrion: You clearly went for woad,[p/] madder,[p/] and weld for your paint.[p/] I'm also guessing you used a touch of gall ink for the shadows?
Tyrion: They're decent choices if you’re trying to paint a shield,[p/] but they're useless if you’re trying to mimic the complex nooks and edges of plate armor.
He just blinked at me.
Knight: ...Seriously?
Tyrion: Plate doesn’t tint that way.[p/] Real plate catches the ambient light in cold tones.
Tyrion: Your mix was a little too warm.
He shook his head,[p/] wide-eyed.
Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Ruby: ...Cuthbert,[p/] was that stuff about the paint really true?
Tyrion: [i]*chuckle*[/i][p/] No,[p/] I just read it from his mind.
Tyrion: Did you see the look on his face?
Tyrion: I kind of understand why my mom enjoyed fortune-telling so much.
She said nothing in response.[p/] Her eyes were locked on my satchel,[p/] now one meal short.
Tyrion: ...I’ll be fine.[p/] They've probably skipped a lot more meals than I have.
Ruby: In case you’ve forgotten,[p/] they were trying to scam us.
Tyrion: ...If they really wanted to hurt anyone,[p/] they'd throw away that fake armor and just use the sword.
Tyrion: We should be helping our fellow commoners, right?[p/] Isn't that why we practice law?
Ruby: ...Just don’t expect me to share my lunch with you.
Ruby: I spent a good fifteen minutes haggling for those sandwiches.
Tyrion: ...Do we even [i]need[/i] to haggle?
Ruby: It's a matter of principle.[p/] You give anyone an inch,[p/] and they'll take a mile.
We arrived at Kornhaven,[p/] but I smelled the stench before I even spotted the village.
The smell of rotting meat and excrement hung in the air.[p/] I had to fight the urge to retch.
But I did my best to swallow my disgust.[p/] I'd been begging Ms. Tymora to take me on her outings for years.[p/] I couldn't show weakness now.
Ruby: ...Are you okay?
Tyrion: ...I'm fine.[p/] I was just...[p/] surprised.
Ruby: Did you expect the ruling lord to install modern plumbing in a village like this?
Tyrion: ...I guess I never really thought about it.
Ruby: You'll get used to it once you've traveled more.
Ruby: This is just how things are in the outer regions.
It really made me appreciate the clean and sanitary streets of the capital.
There,[p/] the streets and homes were loaded with artificery.
We had clean and running water.[p/] We had lights that didn't rely on oil and flame.
It was as if this village existed in a different time period.
...I reprimanded myself.[p/] I wasn't above this.
The people here were commoners,[p/] just like me.[p/] They only lived like this out of a lack of care from the kingdom.
Ruby: ...Take a left here.[p/] The tavern should be at the edge of town.
Ruby: If the reports are right,[p/] this should be the place.

Ruby: Nowadays,[p/] Flinhart McCoy just uses it to host his rallies.
Flinhart: Hey![p/][s/] You lot![p/][s/] The McCoy Tavern is closed!
The dissident leader stormed towards our carriage as we parked.
He had a roughness to him that matched the state of his tavern.[p/] He was practically growling at us with every word.
He got close to Ms. Tymora’s face,[p/] his feet firmly planted on the ground.
She was unflinching, of course,[p/] standing her ground as he leered at the two of us.
Ruby: ...I take it you’re Flinhart McCoy?
Flinhart: ...What’s it to [i]you[/i]?!

His eyes narrowed for a bit before widening in recognition of the name.
Flinhart: Wait...[p/][s/] Ruby [i]Tymora[/i]?!
Flinhart: Why didn’t you say so?![p/][s/] Come on in!
His tone flipped to jovial in an instant.[p/] Now it was as if he were greeting an old friend.
I was surprised people out here even knew who she was.[p/] I'd have thought her infamy had only spread in the city.
I guess he acknowledged her as an ally.[p/] They both had a common enemy, after all.
Flinhart: Excuse the mess.[p/] We haven't exactly seen a lot of guests.

I raised my eyebrow at her.[p/] This was the first time I'd heard of this "client".

Flinhart: I've got a bunch of empty rooms upstairs.
Ruby: Perfect.[p/] Cuthbert,[p/] stay here for a moment.

How was there no need?[p/] The Eye of Horus could extract anything she needed from him.

text: I brought out a small chessboard from my satchel.[p/] Even after a decade,[p/] it had served me well.
Chess could be played with one person—[p/]something that I’d always taken advantage of.
I had a match with the Duke of Commerce scheduled in a few months.
I wasn't too worried about him,[p/] but I might as well take this opportunity.
Tyrion: Checkmate.
I lost again while playing as white.[p/] That was three times now.
Celeste: Oh...[p/] Hello!
Statistically,[p/] white was more likely to win.[p/] So why was I performing better as black?
Celeste: Um...
Was I becoming overconfident as white because it had an advantage?
Celeste: Hello?
That very advantage could be affecting my choices and making me play suboptimally...
If a personal quirk was making me play like this,[p/] then I needed to identify and correct it.
Celeste: Hey!
Tyrion: A-[p/][s/]Ah!
I nearly toppled out of my seat when she tapped me on the shoulder.
She stepped back and flashed me a soft grin.[p/] Her body swayed playfully,[p/] amused by how flustered I was.
Her posture was unguarded.[p/] It was as if she'd never felt threatened in her life.
She was holding a large exotic sword in her right hand,[p/] but I barely even noticed it.
As her warm and bright aura washed over me,[p/] it pulled away any tension and unease I might have felt.
Tyrion: S-[p/][s/]Sorry...[p/] I was distracted...
Celeste: You know the McCoy Tavern is closed, right?
Celeste: But if you’re okay with keeping things off the books,[p/] I can prepare a room for you.

The mumbling upstairs was becoming more frustrated.
Celeste tilted her head as she looked at the ceiling,[p/] but she soon got distracted by my chessboard.
Celeste: Wait...[p/] This game is chess, isn't it?
Celeste: Are you playing against someone?

Celeste: Oh...
Tyrion: ...Playing alone helps me hone my strategies.[p/] Plenty of professionals do it.
Celeste: Hey,[p/] don’t worry about it.[p/] I’m not the type to shame a man for playing with himself.

She just chuckled as she drank in my reaction.
I looked away from her before clearing my throat.

Celeste: I’ve read about it in some books a while ago.
Celeste: Ugh...[p/] This room is always so dusty...
Inviting herself,[p/] she sat down on the chair opposite mine.
She brushed off some dust from the table with her sleeve,[p/] and began setting up her side of the board.
Celeste: Where does the horse go again...?
Tyrion: ...Checkmate.
It only took two moves...[p/] She'd fallen into a classic trap.
I felt a little guilty,[p/] but my mentor always said that failure was the best teacher.
Celeste: ...Wait, what?![p/][s/] Already?!
Celeste: How did you do that?!
I decided against telling her the name of that mate.
She just grumbled under her breath as we both moved our pieces back into place.
Celeste: So...[p/] What kind of business does your mentor have with my dad?
Tyrion: I'm honestly not sure.[p/] She doesn't really tell me much.

Celeste: ...

Celeste: ...It's fine.
Celeste: ...
She stroked her chin as her hand moved and hesitated over each of her pieces.
...She hadn’t realized that she'd been led into another trap.

She moved her knight into position to attack my queen.[p/] She’d blocked my initial assault on her king,[p/] but...
Tyrion: Checkmate.
A Scholar's Mate this time.

I glanced at the ceiling.[p/] Ms. Tymora and Flinhart's argument was only getting worse.

Celeste: We’re not done![p/][s/] Don’t you dare leave!
A few minutes later,[p/] she returned carrying a gigantic slab of cooked meat on a small plate.
I stared at it for a while,[p/] wondering if she was planning to eat all of that in one sitting.
She began setting up her side of the board with one hand while holding a fork [i]and[/i] knife in the other.
She was somehow twirling both utensils in her right hand to switch between them.
She delicately carved a small bit of meat with the knife before spinning to a fork to bring it to her mouth.
She tilted her head at me and swallowed the bite whole.
Celeste: ...Hey, it's your move.
Tyrion: R-[p/][s/]Right...
For someone who had never played before,[p/] she was quickly improving with each game.
It took me a while before I even noticed,[p/] but she was adapting to my strategies and trying to use them against me.
She caught me unaware a few times.
At the rate she was learning,[p/] she could easily surpass a nationally ranked noble with enough practice.
Tyrion: Checkmate.
...But I’d been playing this game far longer than she had,[p/] and I was ranked far above the average noble.
She just narrowed her eyes at me.
Celeste: How are you always one step ahead of me?!
Celeste: It's like you're reading my mind!
I could,[p/] but I never allowed myself to when playing.

How was she eating this so quickly?
Celeste: How is it?

Flinhart’s expression lit up as his daughter rushed over to him.

I winced at that thought.[p/] I’d completely forgotten that I’d have to wrangle those horses again...
I opened the door and fell onto the bed inside.
It wasn't long before night fell.[p/] Between driving that carriage and dealing with those con artists,[p/] I was exhausted.
I fell asleep,[p/] but I felt uneasiness creep into me as the night went on.
I felt myself being pulled away,[p/] brought elsewhere.
In every direction I looked,[p/] there was nothing—[p/]only the glass-like floor I was standing on.
I saw my reflection in the floor.[p/] With each step I took,[p/] it rippled and shimmered.
I knew that I was dreaming,[p/] but something deep inside me insisted that there was something real about this.
Whispers echoed through the realm.[p/] I spotted the small, dark silhouette of a person in the distance.
She was silently staring at me and murmuring something under her breath.
She repeated the same phrase,[p/] almost like a chant.
She looked practically giddy in anticipation of...[p/] [i]something[/i].
Eris: [j]I've finally found you, heavensborn![/j][p/][p/][p/]
I woke up to the sound of shouting from downstairs.
I clutched at my chest.[p/] My heart was racing,[p/] and I was drenched in sweat.
That was just a nightmare...[p/] I tried to convince myself of that.

Tyrion: ...Celeste?
Tyrion: Celeste?![p/][s/] Where are you?!

Peasant: This ain’t the city, boy.[p/] We like our peace and quiet after sundown.
